trans-unit.source: QGIS is an __open source geoinformation system software__. That means the source code is available for everyone, making QGIS a free application. The entire source code can be viewed and downloaded on https://github.com/qgis/QGIS.
trans-unit.target: QGIS is an __open source geoinformation system software__. That means the source code is available for everyone, making QGIS a free application. The entire source code can be viewed and downloaded on https://github.com/qgis/QGIS.

trans-unit.source: QGIS is a __desktop software__: that means you get a program that opens up on your computer as a window with buttons you can click, forms you can fill out to do tasks, and it's generally a visual interactive experience.
trans-unit.target: QGIS is a __desktop software__: that means you get a program that opens up on your computer as a window with buttons you can click, forms you can fill out to do tasks, and it's generally a visual interactive experience.

trans-unit.source: You may __view, edit, capture and analyze spatial data or create printable maps__ with it. QGIS was created in 2002 and is a project of volunteers. And it is __constantly changing__.
trans-unit.target: You may __view, edit, capture and analyze spatial data or create printable maps__ with it. QGIS was created in 2002 and is a project of volunteers. And it is __constantly changing__.

trans-unit.source: QGIS is backed by a __large community of users__, so it’s easy to find solutions to technical issues by using QGIS forums, blogs, or sub-reddits. The official QGIS community can be found [here](https://qgis.org/en/site/forusers/support.html#support). Additionally, a list of helpful websites can be found on the [wiki here](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_common_errors_and_Issues.md). :::
trans-unit.target: QGIS is backed by a __large community of users__, so it’s easy to find solutions to technical issues by using QGIS forums, blogs, or sub-reddits. The official QGIS community can be found [here](https://qgis.org/en/site/forusers/support.html#support). Additionally, a list of helpful websites can be found on the [wiki here](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_common_errors_and_Issues.md). :::

trans-unit.source: Keep in mind that as QGIS gets developed further, the interface and functions of QGIS are subject to changes. The material written for this platform is referring to QGIS Version 3.36. If the training material is no longer up to date, take a look at the [QGIS Documentation](https://docs.qgis.org/3.34/en/docs/index.html).
trans-unit.target: Keep in mind that as QGIS gets developed further, the interface and functions of QGIS are subject to changes. The material written for this platform is referring to QGIS Version 3.36. If the training material is no longer up to date, take a look at the [QGIS Documentation](https://docs.qgis.org/3.34/en/docs/index.html).

trans-unit.source: QGIS is constantly changing, with new features being added. Because of this, the newer versions can have changes or even bugs (such as crashes). However, there is always a stable version available, which is supported for longer. This version is called __Long-term release (LTR)__.
trans-unit.target: QGIS is constantly changing, with new features being added. Because of this, the newer versions can have changes or even bugs (such as crashes). However, there is always a stable version available, which is supported for longer. This version is called __Long-term release (LTR)__.

trans-unit.source: In QGIS, you create projects where you visualise and manipulate geodata. There are three main workflows you will use: __data collection and creation, data processing, and data visualisation__. Geodata is loaded into QGIS projects which will be represented as layers on a map canvas.
trans-unit.target: In QGIS, you create projects where you visualise and manipulate geodata. There are three main workflows you will use: __data collection and creation, data processing, and data visualisation__. Geodata is loaded into QGIS projects which will be represented as layers on a map canvas.

trans-unit.source: There is a lot of complex math involved in the GIS, but QGIS takes care of it on it's own and you don't need to have a deep understanding of maths and algorithms to use QGIS. __As long as you can use Excel and Powerpoint, you shouldn't have trouble learning how to use QGIS__.
trans-unit.target: There is a lot of complex math involved in the GIS, but QGIS takes care of it on it's own and you don't need to have a deep understanding of maths and algorithms to use QGIS. __As long as you can use Excel and Powerpoint, you shouldn't have trouble learning how to use QGIS__.

trans-unit.source: QGIS offers tools to create your own geodata. For example, with the digitizing tools, you can create points, polygons, and lines with information which can represent spatial information. Furthermore, georeferencing lets you add geographic information to various types of data, such as satellite imagery or hand-drawn maps. You will learn how to create geodata and how to georeference data in __[module 2](/content/Module_2/en_module_2_overview.md)__.
trans-unit.target: QGIS offers tools to create your own geodata. For example, with the digitizing tools, you can create points, polygons, and lines with information which can represent spatial information. Furthermore, georeferencing lets you add geographic information to various types of data, such as satellite imagery or hand-drawn maps. You will learn how to create geodata and how to georeference data in __[module 2](/content/Module_2/en_module_2_overview.md)__.

trans-unit.source: Sometimes working with GIS requires you to go and collect the data in the field. In this case, you can use [web and mobile apps](/content/Wiki/en_web_and_mobile_apps_wiki.md).
trans-unit.target: Sometimes working with GIS requires you to go and collect the data in the field. In this case, you can use [web and mobile apps](/content/Wiki/en_web_and_mobile_apps_wiki.md).

trans-unit.source: QGIS offers a wide range of algorithms to process geodata. In the following modules, you will get to know a number of algorithms that are especially useful for GIS in humanitarian work. You will learn more about data processing and manipulation in [module 2](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_2/en_module_2_overview.html) onwards.
trans-unit.target: QGIS offers a wide range of algorithms to process geodata. In the following modules, you will get to know a number of algorithms that are especially useful for GIS in humanitarian work. You will learn more about data processing and manipulation in [module 2](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_2/en_module_2_overview.html) onwards.

trans-unit.source: QGIS lets you visualise geodata and create maps to communicate information. It does so by assigning symbols and colours to different elements in your geodata. Assigning a symbology to the geodata is one of the main skills you will develop as a GIS-user and a good visualisation of data is immensely useful when communicating insights. You will learn how to assign symbols in [Module 4: Visualisation of Geodata and Map Making](/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_I.md)
trans-unit.target: QGIS lets you visualise geodata and create maps to communicate information. It does so by assigning symbols and colours to different elements in your geodata. Assigning a symbology to the geodata is one of the main skills you will develop as a GIS-user and a good visualisation of data is immensely useful when communicating insights. You will learn how to assign symbols in [Module 4: Visualisation of Geodata and Map Making](/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_I.md)

trans-unit.source: In addition to the algorithms included in the standard installation, QGIS offers plugins which add additional functionality to the QGIS application. These plugins are developed by independent organizations or the QGIS-community. For example, plugins let you connect to online services such as OpenStreetMap, or add more algorithms to process your data. These can be very useful for certain use cases. There are also plugins specifically designed for humanitarian work. You will learn more about plugins in the following modules. If you want to know how to install them, look into the [wiki](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_plugins_wiki.md).
trans-unit.target: In addition to the algorithms included in the standard installation, QGIS offers plugins which add additional functionality to the QGIS application. These plugins are developed by independent organizations or the QGIS-community. For example, plugins let you connect to online services such as OpenStreetMap, or add more algorithms to process your data. These can be very useful for certain use cases. There are also plugins specifically designed for humanitarian work. You will learn more about plugins in the following modules. If you want to know how to install them, look into the [wiki](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_plugins_wiki.md).

trans-unit.source: Opening a new project in QGIS
trans-unit.target: Opening a new project in QGIS

trans-unit.source: Open QGIS like any other program on your computer. The start screen of QGIS usually shows you the projects you worked on recently and the option to create a new project.
trans-unit.target: Open QGIS like any other program on your computer. The start screen of QGIS usually shows you the projects you worked on recently and the option to create a new project.

trans-unit.source: There are __two__ options to create a new project:
trans-unit.target: There are __two__ options to create a new project:

trans-unit.source: A QGIS project file has the format ending `.qgz`.
trans-unit.target: A QGIS project file has the format ending `.qgz`.

trans-unit.source: On the start screen click on `Project Template`.
trans-unit.target: On the start screen click on `Project Template`.

trans-unit.source: In the upper left corner click on `Project` → `New Project`.
trans-unit.target: In the upper left corner click on `Project` → `New Project`.

trans-unit.source: Overview of the QGIS Interface
trans-unit.target: Overview of the QGIS Interface

trans-unit.source: The interface of QGIS is at first glance quite complex. However, once you know all the components you will be able to orientate yourself quickly. Here you can find a description of all components of the interface.
trans-unit.target: The interface of QGIS is at first glance quite complex. However, once you know all the components you will be able to orientate yourself quickly. Here you can find a description of all components of the interface.

trans-unit.source: When you hover with your mouse cursor over icons, text will appear which explains the function of the button.
trans-unit.target: When you hover with your mouse cursor over icons, text will appear which explains the function of the button.

trans-unit.source: __Layers panel:__ The __layers list__ shows __all layers/files__ that are __loaded in the project__. You can show/hide layers and set other properties.
trans-unit.target: __Layers panel:__ The __layers list__ shows __all layers/files__ that are __loaded in the project__. You can show/hide layers and set other properties.

trans-unit.source: __Toolbars:__ __Toolbars__ are shortcuts to execute frequently used commands. For example, there are special toolbars for __vector and raster files__, but also general ones for saving your project, etc. The toolbar contains, among other things, a list of all the commands you can use. The toolbar also contains the __processing toolbox__, which is used later in many of the wiki videos.
trans-unit.target: __Toolbars:__ __Toolbars__ are shortcuts to execute frequently used commands. For example, there are special toolbars for __vector and raster files__, but also general ones for saving your project, etc. The toolbar contains, among other things, a list of all the commands you can use. The toolbar also contains the __processing toolbox__, which is used later in many of the wiki videos.

trans-unit.source: __Map Canvas:__ The __map canvas__ is the __central component__ of every GIS programme. This is where the __geodata__ are displayed. The map view has a projection which does not always have to correspond to the projection of the layers.
trans-unit.target: __Map Canvas:__ The __map canvas__ is the __central component__ of every GIS programme. This is where the __geodata__ are displayed. The map view has a projection which does not always have to correspond to the projection of the layers.

trans-unit.source: __Coordinates and Scale:__ Here you can find information about the scale of the map canvas, as well as read the coordinates of the mouse pointer.
trans-unit.target: __Coordinates and Scale:__ Here you can find information about the scale of the map canvas, as well as read the coordinates of the mouse pointer.

trans-unit.source: __Browser panel:__ The browser panel let's you browse the files on your computer and load datasets into your QGIS project.
trans-unit.target: __Browser panel:__ The browser panel let's you browse the files on your computer and load datasets into your QGIS project.

trans-unit.source: __Search bar:__ Here you can __search for tools and layers__ in QGIS. If you don't know where to find a tool, you can try here. You can also type in coordinates to find them on the map canvas.
trans-unit.target: __Search bar:__ Here you can __search for tools and layers__ in QGIS. If you don't know where to find a tool, you can try here. You can also type in coordinates to find them on the map canvas.

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Exercise: Create a new QGIS project
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Exercise: Create a new QGIS project

trans-unit.source: In your “GIS_Training” folder, create a __subfolder__ called "My_First_Project".
trans-unit.target: In your “GIS_Training” folder, create a __subfolder__ called "My_First_Project".

trans-unit.source: In the top-left corner, click on `Project` → `Save as`, browse to your Projects folder and save the project as "Session_1".
trans-unit.target: In the top-left corner, click on `Project` → `Save as`, browse to your Projects folder and save the project as "Session_1".

trans-unit.source: Click on `Save as`, browse to your Projects folder and save the project as "My_First_Project".
trans-unit.target: Click on `Save as`, browse to your Projects folder and save the project as "My_First_Project".

trans-unit.source: Open your your folder and check the __`.qgz` file__ that you just created.
trans-unit.target: Open your your folder and check the __`.qgz` file__ that you just created.

trans-unit.source: If you see a `*` in the title bar, to the left of the name of your project, this means that the project has changes which are __unsaved__. Since QGIS can crash from time to time, make sure to save your project periodically to avoid losing progress.
trans-unit.target: If you see a `*` in the title bar, to the left of the name of your project, this means that the project has changes which are __unsaved__. Since QGIS can crash from time to time, make sure to save your project periodically to avoid losing progress.

trans-unit.source: In QGIS, __mouse control__ allows users to interact with the map canvas, enabling functions such as panning, zooming, and selecting features.
trans-unit.target: In QGIS, __mouse control__ allows users to interact with the map canvas, enabling functions such as panning, zooming, and selecting features.

trans-unit.source: __Hotkeys__ in the QGIS interface provide convenient shortcuts for various commands, enhancing efficiency and speeding up workflow. You can find all hotkeys below.
trans-unit.target: __Hotkeys__ in the QGIS interface provide convenient shortcuts for various commands, enhancing efficiency and speeding up workflow. You can find all hotkeys below.

trans-unit.source: Pans the map to the selected element
trans-unit.target: Pans the map to the selected element

trans-unit.source: Opens the Dialog to create a new print layout
trans-unit.target: Opens the Dialog to create a new print layout

trans-unit.source: Identify features on the map view by clicking on them
trans-unit.target: Identify features on the map view by clicking on them

trans-unit.source: Select a feature by area or single click
trans-unit.target: Select a feature by area or single click

trans-unit.source: Open Attribute table with selected features only
trans-unit.target: Open Attribute table with selected features only

trans-unit.source: Open the Attribute table with selected features only
trans-unit.target: Open the Attribute table with selected features only

trans-unit.source: Open Attribute table with visible features only
trans-unit.target: Open Attribute table with visible features only

trans-unit.source: Open the Attribute table with visible features only
trans-unit.target: Open the Attribute table with visible features only

trans-unit.source: Navigating on the Map Canvas
trans-unit.target: Navigating on the Map Canvas

trans-unit.source: You can follow the steps below in your own QGIS project. Download [this dataset](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_1/wb_boundaries/wb_countries_admin0_10m.gpkg) and drag and drop the `wb_countries_admin0_10m.gpkg` onto the map canvas. The data set are the official country boundaries by the [World Bank](https://datacatalog.worldbank.org/search/dataset/0038272/World-Bank-Official-Boundaries).
trans-unit.target: You can follow the steps below in your own QGIS project. Download [this dataset](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_1/wb_boundaries/wb_countries_admin0_10m.gpkg) and drag and drop the `wb_countries_admin0_10m.gpkg` onto the map canvas. The data set are the official country boundaries by the [World Bank](https://datacatalog.worldbank.org/search/dataset/0038272/World-Bank-Official-Boundaries).

trans-unit.source: Holding the <kbd>Space</kbd>-button on your keyboard activates the ![](/fig/qgis_pan_map.png) `Pan Map`-tool when you're mouse in on the map canvas. Simply move your mouse while holding <kbd>Space</kbd> and you can move the map view.
trans-unit.target: Holding the <kbd>Space</kbd>-button on your keyboard activates the ![](/fig/qgis_pan_map.png) `Pan Map`-tool when you're mouse in on the map canvas. Simply move your mouse while holding <kbd>Space</kbd> and you can move the map view.

trans-unit.source: To move on the map canvas with your mouse cursor you need to toggle the hand button.
trans-unit.target: To move on the map canvas with your mouse cursor you need to toggle the hand button.

trans-unit.source: You can always move on the map canvas with arrow keys on your keyboard.
trans-unit.target: You can always move on the map canvas with arrow keys on your keyboard.

trans-unit.source: Zooming in the map view
trans-unit.target: Zooming in the map view

trans-unit.source: The easiest way to zoom on Map Canvas is by __scrolling__.
trans-unit.target: The easiest way to zoom on Map Canvas is by __scrolling__.

trans-unit.source: Another way is to use the zoom buttons in the toolbox panel.
trans-unit.target: Another way is to use the zoom buttons in the toolbox panel.

trans-unit.source: Basically, all the functionality, tools and applications of QGIS are organised in the Toolbox. Some Tools have their own toolbars which you can add to your QGIS interface.
trans-unit.target: Basically, all the functionality, tools and applications of QGIS are organised in the Toolbox. Some Tools have their own toolbars which you can add to your QGIS interface.

trans-unit.source: Holding <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Alt</kbd> + <kbd>T</kbd> opens and closes the toolbox.
trans-unit.target: Holding <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Alt</kbd> + <kbd>T</kbd> opens and closes the toolbox.

trans-unit.source: To open the Toolbox in QGIS click on the gearwheel button. Or click on `Processing` → `Toolbox`.
trans-unit.target: To open the Toolbox in QGIS click on the gearwheel button. Or click on `Processing` → `Toolbox`.

trans-unit.source: You can use the search bar to find specific tools.
trans-unit.target: You can use the search bar to find specific tools.

trans-unit.source: There are cases when you want to do something in QGIS but do not know the exact tool. Sometimes it's worth just looking for what you think the name of the tool might be.
trans-unit.target: There are cases when you want to do something in QGIS but do not know the exact tool. Sometimes it's worth just looking for what you think the name of the tool might be.

trans-unit.source: Show and hide displays and toolbars
trans-unit.target: Show and hide displays and toolbars

trans-unit.source: There are toolbars and panels for many different tasks. To avoid an overcrowded interface it is smart to only activate specific toolbars or panels only when you really need them.
trans-unit.target: There are toolbars and panels for many different tasks. To avoid an overcrowded interface it is smart to only activate specific toolbars or panels only when you really need them.

trans-unit.source: To add or remove toolbars from your interface click on `View` → `Toolbars` → Check or uncheck the toolboxes you want to add or remove.
trans-unit.target: To add or remove toolbars from your interface click on `View` → `Toolbars` → Check or uncheck the toolboxes you want to add or remove.

trans-unit.source: To add or remove panels from your interface click on `View` → `Panels` → Check or uncheck the panels you want to add or remove.
trans-unit.target: To add or remove panels from your interface click on `View` → `Panels` → Check or uncheck the panels you want to add or remove.

trans-unit.source: At each toolbar there is a field of two dotted lines. If you move the mouse pointer over it until an arrow cross appears and then hold down the left mouse button, you can move the toolbar. This allows an individualised arrangement of your own tools. By compressing all toolbars into a few lines, the map view window can also be enlarged.
trans-unit.target: At each toolbar there is a field of two dotted lines. If you move the mouse pointer over it until an arrow cross appears and then hold down the left mouse button, you can move the toolbar. This allows an individualised arrangement of your own tools. By compressing all toolbars into a few lines, the map view window can also be enlarged.

trans-unit.source: To save progress or to open an existing project in QGIS is very similar to programs like MS Word. However, there is one __BIG__ difference. In QGIS, the geodata you work with is __not__ saved in your QGIS project file. Instead, the project file only contains the file paths where the geodata were located at the time the project was last saved on the PC. If the location of this geodata is subsequently changed, the error message "handle unavailable layers" will appear when the project is opened again.
trans-unit.target: To save progress or to open an existing project in QGIS is very similar to programs like MS Word. However, there is one __BIG__ difference. In QGIS, the geodata you work with is __not__ saved in your QGIS project file. Instead, the project file only contains the file paths where the geodata were located at the time the project was last saved on the PC. If the location of this geodata is subsequently changed, the error message "handle unavailable layers" will appear when the project is opened again.

trans-unit.source: Good data organisation with a fixed and well-thought-out folder structure prevents such problems.
trans-unit.target: Good data organisation with a fixed and well-thought-out folder structure prevents such problems.

trans-unit.source: Holding <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>O</kbd> also opens the `Open Project`-dialogue box.
trans-unit.target: Holding <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>O</kbd> also opens the `Open Project`-dialogue box.

trans-unit.source: To open an existing QGIS project click on `Project` → `Open…` -> Navigate to your project and open it.
trans-unit.target: To open an existing QGIS project click on `Project` → `Open…` -> Navigate to your project and open it.

trans-unit.source: :::{tip} Pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>S</kbd> saves the project, whereas pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>S</kbd> let's you specify a save location on your computer.
trans-unit.target: :::{tip} Pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>S</kbd> saves the project, whereas pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>S</kbd> let's you specify a save location on your computer.

trans-unit.source: __When you save for the first time__: To save the QGIS project you are working on click on `Project` → `Save as…` → Navigate to the folder where you want to save the project → Give the project a name → `Save`.
trans-unit.target: __When you save for the first time__: To save the QGIS project you are working on click on `Project` → `Save as…` → Navigate to the folder where you want to save the project → Give the project a name → `Save`.

trans-unit.source: __When saving your progress__: To save progress in a project that was already saved somewhere on your computer:
trans-unit.target: __When saving your progress__: To save progress in a project that was already saved somewhere on your computer:

trans-unit.source: It can be that while working with QGIS, you come across orange warning icons. This indicates that you should pay attention. To understand what the warning icon means, __hover over it with your mouse__ and explanatory text will appear. For example, in {numref}`warning_icon_example`, the warning icon indicates that the units of measurements are degrees, which are not constant (the distance between 1⁰ of longitude is much greater at the equator than at the poles).
trans-unit.target: It can be that while working with QGIS, you come across orange warning icons. This indicates that you should pay attention. To understand what the warning icon means, __hover over it with your mouse__ and explanatory text will appear. For example, in {numref}`warning_icon_example`, the warning icon indicates that the units of measurements are degrees, which are not constant (the distance between 1⁰ of longitude is much greater at the equator than at the poles).

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Connect with us! :class: tip If you have more questions before or after the training or require assistance, do not hesitate to reach out to us by writing an email to `gis-training-platform@heigit.org`. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Connect with us! :class: tip If you have more questions before or after the training or require assistance, do not hesitate to reach out to us by writing an email to `gis-training-platform@heigit.org`. :::

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Common errors and issues :class: tip We have collected a list of __[Common errors and issues](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_common_errors_and_Issues.html)__. If you ever find yourself at your wits end (which can happen a lot when working with QGIS!), try finding the solution to your problem here. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Common errors and issues :class: tip We have collected a list of __[Common errors and issues](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_common_errors_and_Issues.html)__. If you ever find yourself at your wits end (which can happen a lot when working with QGIS!), try finding the solution to your problem here. :::

trans-unit.source: There is also a big and vibrant QGIS community online. If you are struggling with a specific function, or have questions on how to achieve GIS operations that are not covered on this platform, you can find help on dedicated QGIS forums:
trans-unit.target: There is also a big and vibrant QGIS community online. If you are struggling with a specific function, or have questions on how to achieve GIS operations that are not covered on this platform, you can find help on dedicated QGIS forums:

trans-unit.source: QGIS user forum on stackexchange: https://gis.stackexchange.com/?tags=qgis
trans-unit.target: QGIS user forum on stackexchange: https://gis.stackexchange.com/?tags=qgis

trans-unit.source: Additionally, there is a large amount of youtube tutorials, online guides and learning material for specific GIS-operations, so it is always a good idea to do a quick google search. Amongst others, the [QGIS documentation](https://docs.qgis.org/3.34/en/docs/index.html) also offers Exercises and Training Material, as well as a [Gentle Introduction to QGIS](https://docs.qgis.org/3.34/en/docs/gentle_gis_introduction/index.html).
trans-unit.target: Additionally, there is a large amount of youtube tutorials, online guides and learning material for specific GIS-operations, so it is always a good idea to do a quick google search. Amongst others, the [QGIS documentation](https://docs.qgis.org/3.34/en/docs/index.html) also offers Exercises and Training Material, as well as a [Gentle Introduction to QGIS](https://docs.qgis.org/3.34/en/docs/gentle_gis_introduction/index.html).

trans-unit.source: Take a moment to recapitulate what you've learned in this chapter by answering the questions below:
trans-unit.target: Take a moment to recapitulate what you've learned in this chapter by answering the questions below:

trans-unit.source: __What is QGIS, and what does it mean that it is “open source”?__
trans-unit.target: __What is QGIS, and what does it mean that it is “open source”?__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer QGIS is a Geographic Information System (GIS) software that lets users view, edit, analyze, and produce maps from spatial (geographic) and attribute data. Being open source means that its source code is freely available: anyone can inspect, modify, and distribute it (under license). It also implies that there is a community of users and developers contributing to it, and that the software is generally available at no cost. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer QGIS is a Geographic Information System (GIS) software that lets users view, edit, analyze, and produce maps from spatial (geographic) and attribute data. Being open source means that its source code is freely available: anyone can inspect, modify, and distribute it (under license). It also implies that there is a community of users and developers contributing to it, and that the software is generally available at no cost. :::

trans-unit.source: __Name three things you can do working with QGIS.__
trans-unit.target: __Name three things you can do working with QGIS.__

trans-unit.source: Load spatial data (vector or raster), visualize it on a map
trans-unit.target: Load spatial data (vector or raster), visualize it on a map

trans-unit.source: Edit or digitize features (e.g. adding points, lines, polygons)
trans-unit.target: Edit or digitize features (e.g. adding points, lines, polygons)

trans-unit.source: Perform spatial analysis (e.g. buffer, overlay, join), or produce map outputs (export to PDF/image) :::
trans-unit.target: Perform spatial analysis (e.g. buffer, overlay, join), or produce map outputs (export to PDF/image) :::

trans-unit.source: __How do you know if a QGIS-project has been saved or not?__
trans-unit.target: __How do you know if a QGIS-project has been saved or not?__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer Typically, QGIS will show an asterisk (*) next to the project name or in the window/tab title if there are unsaved changes. If no asterisk appears (or if “Save” is greyed out), that suggests the project is already saved (i.e. up to date). :::
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer Typically, QGIS will show an asterisk (*) next to the project name or in the window/tab title if there are unsaved changes. If no asterisk appears (or if “Save” is greyed out), that suggests the project is already saved (i.e. up to date). :::

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer You can open a QGIS project via the `File` menu → `Open Project` (or “Open Project” button in toolbar), then browse for a `.qgs` or `.qgz` file. Alternatively, double-clicking the project file (if associated on your system) may also open it in QGIS. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer You can open a QGIS project via the `File` menu → `Open Project` (or “Open Project” button in toolbar), then browse for a `.qgs` or `.qgz` file. Alternatively, double-clicking the project file (if associated on your system) may also open it in QGIS. :::

trans-unit.source: __How do you show and hide panels or toolbars?__
trans-unit.target: __How do you show and hide panels or toolbars?__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer In the top bar, use the `View` menu → `Panels` or `Toolbars` submenus to toggle (check/uncheck) specific panels or toolbars. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer In the top bar, use the `View` menu → `Panels` or `Toolbars` submenus to toggle (check/uncheck) specific panels or toolbars. :::

trans-unit.source: __Where can you find help when you are encountering problems with QGIS?__
trans-unit.target: __Where can you find help when you are encountering problems with QGIS?__

trans-unit.source: By searching for tutorials on youtube
trans-unit.target: By searching for tutorials on youtube
